Home /
Bank accounts

Bank accounts

Open a bank account that best supports your financial goals.

Explore the possibilities. From security features to time-saving tools, everything you need is a click away. Open your bank account online in under five minutes.


Find the bank account that’s right for you.

Checking accounts

Easily access and manage funds, track everyday expenses, set up direct deposit and more.

View checking accounts

Savings accounts

Reach your short- and long-term goals by earning steady interest in a savings account.

View savings accounts

Certificate of deposit accounts

Set aside money you don't need access to and see your savings grow faster.

View certificate of deposit accounts

Enjoy U.S. Bank perks no matter which bank account you choose.

Digital tools

Bank on the go with the U.S. Bank Mobile App and online banking. Transfer funds between accounts with ease. Use bill pay, track spending, send money with Zelle®, set savings goals and more.

Security

You’re protected from fraud by the industry’s strongest security measures, our zero-liability policy1 and other features.

Convenience

Access your accounts via about 4,500 U.S. Bank ATMs across the country. Or get help from an experienced banker at one of over 2,000 branch locations.

Level up your bank accounts with the Mobile App.

Stay up to date with your account activity.

View balances and manage your accounts with ease. Set billing reminders, low balance alerts and more with custom notifications.

Move money easily & securely.

Send and receive money, set up transfers or recurring payments, and skip the bank with mobile check deposits all from the app.

Track your spending.

Keep an eye on your spending with a personal spending tracker. Follow spending trends, view your activity, and monitor upcoming bills and expenses so you can budget accordingly.

Set savings goals.

Organize your savings goals by category for easy tracking. Create automated transfers at a cadence that you choose.

Download the app

Get real-time bank account support with cobrowse and more.

Schedule time with one of our experienced bankers online, on the phone or in person to get help with a bank account.

Schedule an appointment with a banker


Online appointments include cobrowse, allowing our banker to securely view your screen and assist you with setting up your account, managing your finances and more. You can start a cobrowse session from anywhere with just one click for immediate support.

See how cobrowse works

Explore our special banking programs and services.

Military banking

With resources and exclusive benefits, we’re committed to helping military veterans achieve their financial goals.

Learn more about military banking Learn how we support the community

Student banking

Open a bank account as a young adult under 24 and enjoy benefits designed for you, plus tools and services to help you manage your finances with confidence.

Learn about student banking perks

Goals coaching

Looking for account support? Not sure which bank account works for you? Our goals coaches can help. You’ll meet with your coach online or in person and discuss your goals – financial or not. Best of all, it’s on us.

Get to know our coaches

Frequently asked questions

You can open a bank account online or in a branch if you’re 18 years or older and a legal U.S. resident. To open an account online, you’ll need:

  • Your Social Security number.
  • A minimum opening deposit to activate your account (once you’ve been approved). This can be paid with a credit, debit or prepaid card, a transfer from another U.S. Bank account or a transfer from another financial institution.

If you prefer to visit a U.S. Bank branch, you’ll need personal details such as a valid government ID. Feel free to call a U.S. Bank representative at 855-605-1871 to ensure you have what you need.

With a bank account, you can:

  • Conveniently and safely access your money using a debit card, ATM or checks without needing to carry cash.
  • Monitor your account, transfer funds, pay bills online, track spending, budget your money and more via digital tools.
  • Easily set up direct deposit of your paycheck or other recurring payments, saving a trip to the bank and giving you immediate access to funds.
  • Get covered with the standard deposit insurance coverage limit, which is $250,000 per depositor, per ownership category, per FDIC-insured bank.

Accessing your account online is safe and secure. Our industry-leading encryption and security features are always on to protect you and your information.

  • Digital security guarantee: You aren’t liable for any unauthorized transactions made with the mobile app or online banking.1
  • Security center: Boost the security of your accounts in minutes by easily enabling security features within the mobile app and online banking.
  • Data privacy: Share your financial information only with those you choose.
  • Custom account notifications: Monitor your balances and activity, your way. There’s no need to sign up for alerts that inform you of important activity, like requests to change your password.2 Learn more about limiting and reporting unauthorized transfers.

A minor can only open a bank account with an adult.

If you want to open a Bank Smartly Checking® account with a minor (aged 13 through 17), you can open the account online or in a branch, as long as you’re both present. If opening the account online, you’ll need the teen’s Social Security number, income (if any) and a few other details.

If the minor is under the age of 13, or if you’d like to open any other type of bank account with a minor aside from the above, you’ll need to make an appointment and go to a branch together. Get more tips for teaching kids about money.

Learn how to open a bank account for a minor

You can apply for an individual account if you’re 18 years or older and a legal U.S. resident. You’ll need to provide your Social Security number and a valid, government-issued photo ID. Find out how to open your first bank account.

You can usually access your bank account immediately after it's opened and funded. Depending on the account, you may be able to view balances, transfer money, pay bills, and set up direct deposits.

Understanding the difference between checking and savings accounts is key to finding the right one for you. A checking account is typically used for day-to-day spending and paying your bills. You can easily access your money with a debit card, ATM or check. A savings account is meant to help you grow your money or set aside funds for a big purchase. It’s often beneficial to have both as part of your financial plan.

 

Our do-it-yourself automated direct deposit setup makes it easy and takes just a few minutes in the Mobile App or online. Your direct deposit update is seamless, secure and verified in real time. You can do this for paychecks, Social Security benefits and more. (For other federal benefits, visit godirect.gov.)

Whether you’re opening a new account or want to add a beneficiary to an existing one, we’ll need to know the type of account (checking, savings, CD) and some personal information about your beneficiary. You’ll also need to provide written confirmation of your request via signature.

Head to a U.S. Bank branch or call 800-USBANKS (872-2657) to get started. We accept relay calls.

You can find your account number and routing number through any of the following:

  • U.S. Bank Mobile App
  • Online banking 
  • Your monthly statement
  • Your checkbook
  • Our routing number directory

TAKE THE NEXT STEP

Footnote
Return to content, Footnote

Disclosures

  1. Zero fraud liability – U.S. Bank provides zero fraud liability for unauthorized transactions. Cardholder must notify U.S. Bank promptly of any unauthorized use. Certain conditions and limitations may apply. See the Electronic Fund Transfers section in Your Deposit Account Agreement (PDF)  for details.

  2. For text alerts, standard messaging charges apply through your mobile carrier and message frequency depends on account settings. Please be advised that these alerts may not be sent immediately. Visit Cellular phone contact policy for additional information.

Start of disclosure content

Deposit products are offered by U.S. Bank National Association. Member FDIC.

Zelle® and the Zelle® related marks are wholly owned by Early Warning Services, LLC and are used herein under license.
